operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $42.3 billion driven by advertising revenue growth with ad impressions up 5% and average price per ad increasing 10%. Reality Labs revenue declined 6% due to lower Meta Quest sales partially offset by higher Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses sales. Operating cash flows increased to $24.0 billion from $19.3 billion despite user engagement shifting toward lower-monetizing Reels and messaging products.
cost_structure: Total costs and expenses increased 9% to $24.8 billion with research and development expenses rising 22% driven by AI infrastructure investments and 14% headcount growth in engineering functions. Cost of revenue increased 14% primarily from higher data center operational expenses. The company extended estimated useful lives of servers and network assets to 5.5 years, reducing depreciation expense by $826 million and increasing net income by $695 million.
material_events: Meta returned $14.1 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, with quarterly dividend increased to $0.525 per share. Capital expenditures reached $12.9 billion with full-year 2025 projection of $64-72 billion for infrastructure investments. European Commission imposed EUR 998 million in fines for Facebook Marketplace practices and Digital Markets Act violations, both under appeal.
macro_exposures: European regulatory environment creates escalating business disruption through DMA enforcement and data transfer restrictions, with potential service limitations and revenue impacts. iOS advertising measurement limitations continue negatively impacting targeting capabilities and marketer spending patterns. Geopolitical conflicts affect advertiser spending across regions, while China-based advertising resellers create revenue concentration risk vulnerable to trade disputes.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued significant AI infrastructure investments and headcount expansion to enhance products and advertising tools, with Reality Labs operating losses projected to increase in 2025. European business model modifications anticipated by Q3 2025 based on regulatory feedback, potentially resulting in materially worse user experience and significant revenue impact. Effective tax rate expected at 12-15% for full year 2025.
uncertainty_profile: FTC antitrust trial begins April 2025 seeking substantial consent order modifications including prohibition on minors' data use and product launch limitations. Multiple ongoing securities litigation includes youth addiction claims with first personal injury trial set for November 2025. Tax contingencies total $10.9 billion with uncertain resolution timing, while pending minimum tax implementations and evolving AI regulatory frameworks create additional compliance complexity.
